free_text: Fission and spallation induced by 7-GeV protons on
U, Bi, Pb, Au, W, Ho, and Ag.
Study of the
sensitivity of the reaction given under 'Monitor' to
the secondary particles.
Muscovite mica sheets with thickness about
150 um, Z threshold about 16;  Makrofol-N with
thickness about 100 um, Z threshold about 8;
Makrofol-E with thickness about 200 um, Z
threshold about 2;  Daicel with thickness about 100 um,
Z threshhold=protons.
Data obtained with muscovite mica detectors.
Data obtained with Makrofol-N detectors.
Data obtained with Makrofol-E detectors.
Data obtained with Daicel detectors.
Layers of natural U, Bi, Pb, Au, W, Ho, and Ag, 10 to
200 um/cm**2 thick, were evaporated into the
detectors.  The targets were sandwiched by using blank
sheets of the same detectors.  Exposures were carried
out at 90 degrees with respect to the detector surface.
Only those tracks with physical lengths greater than
2 um were taken as genuine tracks.
Notperformed: the higher sensitivity of Daicel is
expected to introduce an error in the spallation
cross-section values because of the inclusion of
uncorrelated tracks due to some interactions from
impurities.
Ratio of the number of reaction products
going in the forward direction to those going in the
backward direction.
Spallation cross section. Single unpaired
tracks having projected lengths greater than 2 um
were taken to be caused by spallation reaction products
Uncertainty in the proton flux determination.
No explanations for the quoted errors
Compiler assumes the quoted errors do not include the
uncertainties in the proton flux determination.
Distributions of opening angles between the
two fragments emitted in binary fission.
Tabl. I from Phys.Rev.,C29(1984)2199
By Khlopin's Institute - A.P.
